Scott Warren
About MeBlogs about Homelabs, data, and tech

Work History

AI Data Annotation - Coding Specialist | DataAnnotation.tech

Ad-hoc

Working on ad-hoc projects involving AI prompt-engineering and reinforment learning over a number of areas, with a specialism in coding. Other work within the AI sphere includes creation of ruberics, peer-review, and extensive fact-checking to highlight hallucinations and other AI artifacts.

Business Intelligence Manager | G4S Patient Transport Services

June 2023 – Present

Stragtegic and hands-on leadership of a team of analysts within the healthcare logistics sector. Developed the business’ first dedicated data infrastrucure using BigQuery and ingestion of data from SQL, JSON, REST and other sources. Introduced machine learning approaches using R as a PoC for a wider project involving modern AI platforms. Designed custom ingestion methods within python for REST and SOAP APIs.

Data Analyst | Lloyd Warwick International

July 2022 - June 2023

Data driven loss adjusting, focusing on multimillion dollar claims within the reinsurance and energy markets. Gathered a wide range of data sources using REST APIs, automated ocr ingestion of PDF files and other data sources such as Twitter/X for sentiment analysis. Established a “reproduciblity first” workflow using RMarkdown, creating an auditable and transparent loss adjusting process.

Senior Data Analyst | SEIB Insurance Brokers

July 2021 - June 2022

Utilised R and SQL to perform granular underwriting analysis, turning five historically unprofitable insurance schemes into net-profitable products within 6 months. Managed reporting and analysis of a suite of 20 + delegated authority schemes, presenting results to internal and external stakeholders. Generation of self-service Excel sheets providing “What-if” analytics to underwriters, showing the main leavers to pull in order to change the course of a product.

Edcuation and Qualifications

Foundation Insurance Test (FIT) | Chartered Insurance Institue | 2021

Obtained during employment with SEIB Insurance Brokers

MPhil Environmental Biology | University of Essex | 2020

Part of a £8.3m grant funded by NERC - Macronutrient Cycles

BSc (Hons.) Marine and Freshwater Biology | University of Essex | 2012

Assistant Instructor | PADI | 2008

Publications

Streams of data from drops of water: 21st century molecular microbial ecology

Dave R. Clark, Robert M. W. Ferguson, Danielle N. Harris, Kirsty J. Matthews Nicholass, Hannah J. Prentice, Kate C. Randall, Luli Randell, Scott L. Warren, and Alex J. Dumbrell | WIRES Water (2018)

Explored the transition of ecology into a data-intensive science. Focused on the bioinformatic pipelines and computational frameworks required to process large-scale environmental DNA (eDNA) datasets.

View Source

Gene-to-ecosystem impacts of a catastrophic pesticide spill: testing a multilevel bioassessment approach in a river ecosystem

Murray S. A. Thompson, Claire Bankier, Thomas Bell, Alex J. Dumbrell, Clare Gray, Mark E. Ledger, Katja Lehmann, Boyd A. McKew, Carl D. Sayer, Felicity Shelley, Mark Trimmer, Scott L. Warren, and Guy Woodward | Freshwater Biology (2016)

A multi-year study evaluating the cascading effects of a pesticide spill. This work involved high-dimensional data integration across multiple biological scales, from microbial genes to macroscopic food webs.

View Source
